description_long: This object is a potted Lily of the Valley plant, featuring delicate, nodding white bell-shaped flowers arranged along a gently arching green stem. The flowers are small and hang in a row, each with a flared, scalloped edge, and are attached to the stem by short pedicels. The plant has several broad, lanceolate green leaves that emerge from the base and rise alongside the stem, providing a lush backdrop to the flowers. The plant is set in a round, reddish-brown terracotta pot filled with a light brown soil. The pot has a slightly irregular, hand-crafted appearance with subtle surface texture. The overall look is naturalistic and would be suitable for placement in urban parks, gardens, or on sidewalks.
description: A potted Lily of the Valley with arching green stems, white bell-shaped flowers, and broad green leaves in a terracotta pot.
description_view_0: Front view showing the arching stem with white bell-shaped flowers and two prominent green leaves in a round terracotta pot.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the broad back leaf and the curve of the stem, with fewer flowers visible.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the layered arrangement of flowers and leaves, with the pot's texture visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the dense cluster of flowers and overlapping leaves, with the pot's rim clearly visible.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are fused into a bell-shaped, radial arrangement, forming a symmetrical, nodding corolla with scalloped edges. Each flower is distinct and evenly spaced along the arching stem.
